evidence: Closest SOTA; we extend with write-time AST + behavioural-replay oracle + mutation-grounded false-heal eval.
Their VISTA-733 benchmark is our back-compat baseline; we build the modern-React equivalent ReactHealBench.
Industry case study surfaces 3 regression-masking incidents anecdotally; we formalise that into a measurable axis.
ReactHealBench fills the modern-React benchmark gap.
LLM context includes Fiber tree + TS types + intent, not just DOM.
Behavioural-replay oracle is executable, not lexical.
Repair-success claim is one of two primary axes for IDEA-1.
False-heal claim is THE primary axis.
Both are 2025 LLM-for-test-repair; orthogonal failure classes (NIO vs GUI brittle).

Invalidates IDEA-1's 'no React-native benchmark' novelty claim; ReproBreak already provides 449 Cypress/Playwright locator breaks from 359 repos. IDEA-1 must reposition ReactHealBench as ReproBreak-extension with mutation-grounded false-heal probes, not standalone.

Joseph's 10-tier accessibility ladder is conceptually equivalent to HealReact's L1 deterministic rungs; HealReact's contribution must isolate to (a) the intent-label layer ON TOP of his ladder, (b) the behavioural-replay oracle, (c) the false-heal eval — none of which Joseph addresses.
UTFix's static-slice+dynamic-slice+failure-message context-pack pattern informs HealReact L3's LLM context design.
TRaf solves async-wait timeout repair specifically; HealReact L3's 'wait_strategy' patch type should reuse or compare against TRaf for that single failure class.
